front: Poem: opens "If ever two were one, then surely we"; speaker prizes love "more than whole Mines of gold, / Or all the riches that the East doth hold"
back: "To My Dear and Loving Husband" (Anne Bradstreet)
work: To My Dear and Loving Husband

front: Poem: addresses a poetry collection as "thou ill-form'd offspring of my feeble brain" and a "rambling brat" — it was published without the author's knowledge by John Woodbridge
Poem: speaker awakened by "thund'ring noise / And piteous shrieks" during a 1666 fire; concludes "My hope and treasure lies above"
back: "Verses upon the Burning of Our House" (Anne Bradstreet)
work: Verses upon the Burning of Our House

front: Poem: opens "To sing of Wars, of Captaines, and of Kings" — claims these are "too superior things" for the author's "mean pen"; stanza 5: "my hand a needle better fits"
back: "The Prologue" to The Tenth Muse (Anne Bradstreet)

front: Author: subject of a 57-stanza "Homage" by John Berryman that asks "moved you not, restless, waiting for him?"
Poem: New England tells Old England "Farewell, dear mother; Parliament, prevail, / And in a while you'll tell another tale"
back: "A Dialogue between Old England and New" (Anne Bradstreet)
